channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: National probe uncovers over 50,000 suspected fake birth certificates in Philippines
item.description: Ang National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) ay nagsalita tungkol sa pag-identify ng mahigit 50,000 potensyal na pekeng sertipiko ng kapanganakan na nakarehistro sa mahigit 1,600 mga lokal na opisina ng...

item.title: Manila Mayor Isko Moreno Announces Bid for Reelection in 2028, Focuses on 10-Year Development Plan
item.description: Manila Mayor Isko Moreno has confirmed his intention to run for reelection as city mayor in 2028, putting an end to speculations that he might pursue a higher national position. In a recent interview,...

item.title: Rising Political Violence Across Nigeria Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: As we dey enter the period for political parties to start their primaries, tension don dey everywhere for Nigeria. Reports show say harassment of political opponents, thuggery, physical attacks, and d...

item.title: Delhi Experiences Unusual Fog and Temperature Drop in March
item.description: Delhiites woke up to a rather surprising sight this Saturday – a thick blanket of fog! It's quite unusual to see this kind of weather in Delhi towards the end of March, which has already been marked b...

item.title: Authorities monitor stranded vessel as Cyclone Narelle cleanup continues
item.description: Cyclone Narelle has caused significant disruption across the Cape York Peninsula, leaving around 1,000 homes without power and an unidentified vessel stranded in Princess Charlotte Bay. The storm hit ...

item.title: Liverpool Condemns Racism After Konate Targeted Following Osimhen Injury
item.description: Liverpool Football Club don talk say dem dey highly upset about di racial abuse wey Ibahima Konate, dia defender, receive online after Victor Osimhen, Super Eagles striker, fracture im forearm. Di inc...

item.title: Experts advocate for greater female participation in resistance training through improved gym environments and education
item.description: Emerging research highlights significant health benefits from resistance training for women, potentially even surpassing those for men in certain areas. The National Institutes of Health recommends ev...

item.title: US-led Alliance Expands Military Manufacturing in Asia, Including Possible Ammunition Facility in the Philippines
item.description: The United States and its allies are expanding military manufacturing in the Asia-Pacific region to strengthen defense capabilities near potential conflict zones. The Pentagon announced that the Partn...

item.title: Pakistan Sees Surge in Digital Payments, Reaching 92% of Retail Transactions
item.description: A recent report reveals a significant shift in Pakistan's retail payment landscape, with digital channels now accounting for a whopping 92% of all transactions. This marks a substantial increase, indi...

item.title: US Considers Scaling Back Iran Military Operations While Ceasefire Remains Unlikely
item.description: President Donald Trump announced on Friday that the United States is thinking about winding down military operations against Iran, but he clarified that a formal ceasefire is not being sought. Meanwhi...

item.title: Deepa Anappara’s ‘The Last of Earth’ Explores Desire, History, and Colonial Tibet
item.description: Deepa Anappara’s novel, ‘The Last of Earth’, is a compelling historical fiction set against the backdrop of the Great Game between Britain and Russia in Central Asia during the late 19th century. The ...

item.title: Pakistan’s Political Unity: Challenges and Realities in 2026
item.description: The article by Nadeem F. Paracha in Dawn examines the ongoing debate about political unity in Pakistan in 2026. Some analysts have suggested that reconciliation between the military, the government, a...

item.title: Balochistan universities face shortage of senior faculty due to migration
item.description: QUETTA: Balochistan Governor Jaffar Khan Mandokhail highlighted on Thursday that the province's public universities are facing a serious shortage of senior faculty due to brain drain. According to him...

item.title: PM Modi and President Murmu extend Eid-ul-Fitr greetings; festivities commence across India
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi extended his heartfelt greetings to the nation on the occasion of Eid-ul-Fitr via social media on Saturday. In his message, he wished everyone happiness and health, emphas...

item.title: Leaders Emphasise Unity and Compassion as Pakistan Celebrates Eid ul Fitr Nationwide
item.description: Eid ul Fitr is being celebrated all over Pakistan with great devotion and joy, marking the end of the holy month of Ramadan. Across the country, large congregations gathered in mosques, Eidgahs, and o...

item.title: Study Links Fusobacterium nucleatum from Gum Disease to Promotion of Breast Cancer in Preclinical Models
item.description: Researchers from Johns Hopkins Medicine have uncovered a potential connection between a common oral bacterium associated with periodontal disease, Fusobacterium nucleatum, and breast cancer developmen...

item.title: Tanzania Strengthens Development Partnership with Canada
item.description: Tanzania's Minister of Finance, Ambassador Khamis Mussa Omar, has reaffirmed the government's strong commitment to deepening development cooperation with Canada. During a productive meeting held at th...

item.title: Minister Makonda Highlights Importance of Road Development to AFCON Stadium in Arusha
item.description: Dar es Salaam – On March 20, 2026, Minister for Information, Arts, Culture, and Sports, Paul Makonda, attended the official ceremony for the construction of roads leading to the upcoming AFCON 2027 St...

item.title: Calls for Justice After Sexual Assaults During Ozoro Cultural Festival
item.description: Plenty wahala don burst after viral videos show young women and girls dey harassed and molested during one cultural festival for Ozoro, Isoko North LGA, Delta State. People wey dey concerned, includin...

item.title: French nuclear plant employee dies from meningitis; no connection to UK outbreak
item.description: An employee at the French nuclear fuel company Orano, based at its La Hague site in Normandy, has passed away due to meningitis. The company clarified that the case is not linked to the ongoing mening...
